leads.name: Copper Canyon Press
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry press founded 1972. Port Townsend, WA. 15-20 titles/yr. Distributed by Consortium/Ingram. One of the most respected poetry publishers in the US. CLMP member.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry press since 1972 -- 15 to 20 titles per year, distributed by Consortium, and consistently ranked among the most respected poetry publishers in the country.

leads.name: BOA Editions
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry press founded 1976. Rochester, NY. 12-15 titles/yr. National Book Award and Pulitzer Prize winners in catalog. Distributed by Consortium. CLMP member.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry press since 1976 in Rochester, NY -- 12 to 15 titles per year with National Book Award and Pulitzer Prize winners in the catalog, distributed by Consortium.

leads.name: Four Way Books
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press, NYC. Founded 1991. ~10 titles/yr of poetry and literary fiction. Active competition program (Intro Prize, Levis Prize). CLMP member. Strong critical reputation.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press in New York since 1991 -- ~10 titles per year of poetry and literary fiction, with an active competition program that creates a predictable annual production cycle.

leads.name: University of Akron Press
leads.notes: AUPresses member. Akron, OH -- 60 miles from Ann Arbor. ~12-15 titles/yr: poetry series (Akron Poetry Prize), Ohio/Midwest history, technology studies. Strong Midwest institutional presence.
leads.pitch: AUPresses institutional publisher based in Akron, Ohio -- 60 miles from Ann Arbor -- with 12 to 15 titles per year including the Akron Poetry Prize series, Ohio and Midwest history, and technology studies.

leads.name: Northern Illinois University Press
leads.notes: AUPresses member. DeKalb, IL -- approx. 4 hours from Ann Arbor. ~15-20 titles/yr: political science, history, Southeast Asia studies, Midwest regional. Institutional budget stability.
leads.pitch: AUPresses institutional publisher in DeKalb, Illinois -- about 4 hours from Ann Arbor -- with 15 to 20 titles per year across political science, history, Southeast Asia studies, and Midwest regional topics.

leads.name: Coffee House Press
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press founded 1984. Minneapolis, MN. ~15-20 titles/yr. Poetry, fiction, essay. National Book Award, NBCC, and PEN Award winners. Distributed by Consortium. CLMP.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press since 1984 in Minneapolis -- 15 to 20 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and essays, with National Book Award and PEN Award winners throughout the catalog.

leads.name: Cleveland State University Poetry Center
leads.notes: University poetry press, Cleveland, OH -- 2.5 hours from Ann Arbor. ~6-8 titles/yr. CSU Poetry Center Prize. Established 1971. Strong regional and national profile.
leads.pitch: University poetry press at CSU in Cleveland, Ohio -- 2.5 hours from Ann Arbor -- publishing 6 to 8 titles per year since 1971, including the annual CSU Poetry Center Prize.

leads.name: New Rivers Press
leads.notes: Literary press at Minnesota State University Moorhead. Founded 1968. ~8-10 titles/yr. Poetry and fiction. American Fiction Prize. 55+ year track record. CLMP member.
leads.pitch: Literary press at Minnesota State Moorhead, founded 1968 -- one of the oldest continuously operating literary presses in the country at 55+ years, publishing 8 to 10 titles per year of poetry and fiction.

leads.name: Ahsahta Press
leads.notes: Boise State University poetry press. Boise, ID. Founded 1974. ~6-8 titles/yr. Focus on innovative and experimental poetry. Sawtooth Poetry Prize. 50-year track record.
leads.pitch: Boise State University poetry press since 1974 -- 6 to 8 titles per year of innovative and experimental poetry, with the Sawtooth Poetry Prize drawing national submissions and creating a steady annual publication program.

leads.name: Ugly Duckling Presse
leads.notes: Nonprofit artists' book and poetry press, Brooklyn, NY. Founded 2001. ~8-12 titles/yr. Handmade and offset editions. Translations, poetry, experimental prose. CLMP. Critical reputation.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit artists' book and poetry press in Brooklyn since 2001 -- 8 to 12 titles per year of poetry, translations, and experimental prose, with a distinctive mix of handmade and offset editions.

leads.name: Etruscan Press
leads.notes: Independent literary press, Wilkes-Barre, PA. Founded 2001. ~8-10 titles/yr. Poetry, fiction, and literary nonfiction. National distribution. P&W listed. Strong award recognition.
leads.pitch: Independent literary press in Wilkes-Barre, PA since 2001 -- 8 to 10 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and literary nonfiction with strong award recognition and national distribution.

leads.name: Acre Books
leads.notes: Literary press at University of Cincinnati. Cincinnati, OH -- ~3 hours from Ann Arbor. Founded 2016. ~6-8 titles/yr. Literary fiction and poetry. Strong national profile for a newer press.
leads.pitch: University of Cincinnati-housed literary press since 2016 -- 6 to 8 titles per year of literary fiction and poetry from Cincinnati, Ohio, about 3 hours from Ann Arbor.

leads.name: Tilbury House Publishers
leads.notes: Independent children's and nature/nonfiction publisher, Thomaston, ME. Founded 1990. ~10 titles/yr. Science, nature, social issues for young readers. Strong school and library market.
leads.pitch: Independent children's and nature publisher in Maine since 1990 -- approximately 10 titles per year focused on science, nature, and social issues for young readers, with strong school and library distribution.

leads.name: Arte Publico Press
leads.notes: University of Houston-housed press. Founded 1979. ~20-25 titles/yr. Largest US publisher of Hispanic literature. AUPresses member. Recovers Historical Memory project. Strong institutional backing.
leads.pitch: University of Houston-housed press since 1979 -- the largest publisher of US Hispanic literature, with 20 to 25 titles per year and AUPresses membership providing institutional budget stability.

leads.name: Fence Books
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry and fiction press, Albany, NY. Founded 2001. ~5-8 titles/yr. Associated with Fence magazine. Experimental and challenging work. CLMP. National Intro Prize.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry and fiction press in Albany, NY since 2001 -- 5 to 8 titles per year of experimental and challenging literary work, associated with Fence magazine and operating the National Intro Prize.

leads.name: Pleiades Press
leads.notes: University of Central Missouri literary press and journal. Warrensburg, MO. ~4-6 titles/yr (book arm). Long-running literary journal alongside book program. P&W listed.
leads.pitch: University of Central Missouri-based literary press and journal -- 4 to 6 book titles per year of poetry and fiction, alongside a long-running literary journal that gives the press national visibility.

leads.name: Word Works
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry publisher, Washington DC. Founded 1974. ~4-6 titles/yr. Washington Prize, Tenth Gate Prize. 50-year track record. P&W listed.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry publisher in Washington DC since 1974 -- 4 to 6 titles per year including the Washington Prize and Tenth Gate Prize, with a 50-year track record of supporting American poetry.

leads.name: Sundress Publications
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press, Knoxville, TN. ~6-10 titles/yr. Poetry and short fiction. Chapbooks and full collections. Best of the Net Anthology. P&W listed. Growing national profile.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press in Knoxville, TN -- 6 to 10 titles per year of poetry and short fiction including chapbooks and full collections, with a growing national profile and the Best of the Net Anthology.

leads.name: Moon City Press
leads.notes: Missouri State University literary press. Springfield, MO. ~4-6 titles/yr. Poetry and fiction. Moon City Poetry Award. University-housed with institutional support. P&W listed.
leads.pitch: Missouri State University literary press in Springfield, MO -- 4 to 6 titles per year of poetry and fiction, with the Moon City Poetry Award creating a reliable annual production cycle.

leads.name: Conduit Books & Ephemera
leads.notes: Minneapolis-based small literary press. ~3-5 titles/yr. Poetry and experimental work. Associated with Conduit magazine. CLMP member. Highly curated, distinctive physical design.
leads.pitch: Minneapolis-based literary press -- 3 to 5 titles per year of poetry and experimental work, associated with Conduit magazine and known for distinctive physical design and highly curated selections.
